title: The data black hole at the center of AI
summary: Dwarkesh Patel argues that massive data volume is the invisible force holding together AI capabilities, comparing AI models to galaxies with a central "data black hole." The essay explores the scale of training data, human versus AI sample efficiency, and whether sample efficiency improvements matter for progress.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel argues that the scale of training data, rather than sample efficiency, is the central driver behind current AI model capabilities. He contrasts human and AI learning efficiency to point at a fundamental asymmetry: models lean on enormous token counts to reach their capabilities, while humans learn from far less. The framing matters because it locates the engine of recent progress in data scale and scaling rather than in efficient learning, which has implications for how trajectories are read.
